description_long: This is a two-story brick house with a symmetrical facade and a hip roof covered in reddish-brown tiles. The exterior walls are constructed from red brick with white stone trim around the windows and corners, giving it a classic, sturdy appearance. The front features a prominent blue door set beneath a stone overhang supported by decorative brackets, flanked by two large windows on either side. The upper story has three evenly spaced windows, all framed in white, maintaining the building's balanced look. The roof is gently sloped on all sides, and the overall structure is rectangular with clean, straight lines. The house exudes a traditional, early 20th-century urban residential style, suitable for placement in a city neighborhood.
description: A symmetrical two-story red brick house with white trim, a blue front door, and a hip tile roof. The facade features evenly spaced windows and a decorative stone entryway.
description_view_0: Front view showing the blue main door, stone overhang, and three upper windows with white trim.
description_view_1: Side view with no visible door, showing two lower and two upper windows, all symmetrically arranged.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the side, with two lower and two upper windows, no entryway visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, mirroring the other side, with symmetrical window placement and no door.
attribute_entryway_design: Central blue door with stone overhang, decorative brackets, and white trim
